message:
feat(scheduler,audit): typed cap waiters with reset abort, and audit DB connection hardening

Scheduler (caps.go):
- Replace raw `chan struct{}` waiters with typed agentCapWaiter struct
  that carries a `granted` flag alongside the channel. This lets
  Reset() distinguish between "slot freed normally" and "reset aborted
  your wait," preventing a race where a goroutine could falsely believe
  it acquired a slot after a concurrent Reset().
- Add errAgentCapReset sentinel error so callers can distinguish
  capacity-reset aborts from context cancellation.
- Pre-check ctx.Err() at Acquire() entry to fail fast when the context
  is already done, avoiding unnecessary lock acquisition.

Scheduler (scheduler.go):
- Adjustments to integrate the new typed waiter infrastructure and
  propagate reset errors through the scheduling pipeline.

Audit (audit.go):
- Add busy_timeout(5000) and foreign_keys(1) pragmas to the audit
  SQLite connection, preventing SQLITE_BUSY under concurrent audit
  writes and enabling referential integrity.
- Set MaxOpenConns(1) and MaxIdleConns(1) to serialize audit DB access,
  which is appropriate for the append-only audit workload and prevents
  WAL contention from pooled connections.

Tests:
- caps_test.go: 87 lines of tests covering waiter grant/reset semantics,
  context pre-cancellation, and concurrent acquire/release.
- scheduler_test.go: 83 lines of tests for the updated scheduling flow.
- audit_test.go: 22 lines of tests for the hardened DB configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
